title: why `podman container ls` not like `crictl ps -a`
body:
**Description**

Why the container list of podman on the same host is different from crio

**Describe the results you received:**
```sh
➜ crictl ps -a  
CONTAINER ID        IMAGE               CREATED             STATE               NAME                ATTEMPT             POD ID
d4aee87532ecd       busybox             9 minutes ago       Running             busybox             0                   0d41ba2e5580f
```

```sh
➜ podman container ls
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE  COMMAND  CREATED  STATUS  PORTS  NAMES
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```sh
➜ podman container ls
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE  COMMAND  CREATED  STATUS  PORTS  NAMES
d4aee87532ecd       busybox             9 minutes ago       Running             busybox             0                   0d41ba2e5580f
```

**Output of `crio --version`:**

```sh
➜ crio --version
crio version 1.16.3
commit: "9aad8e4832d2e1d03db6e54bdd5a3dbf72c021c8-dirty"
```

```sh
➜ podman version     
Version:            1.4.4
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.8
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

```sh

    hooks_dir = ["/usr/share/containers/oci/hooks.d"]
    default_mounts = []
    default_capabilities = ["CHOWN", "DAC_OVERRIDE", "FSETID", "FOWNER", "SETGID", "SETUID", "SETPCAP", "NET_BIND_SERVICE", "KILL"]
    default_sysctls = []

    conmon_cgroup = "system.slice"

    cgroup_manager = "systemd"
    default_mounts_file = ""
    container_exits_dir = "/var/run/crio/exits"
    container_attach_socket_dir = "/var/run/crio"
    bind_mount_prefix = ""

    gid_mappings = ""

    manage_network_ns_lifecycle = false
    read_only = false
    [crio.runtime.runtimes]
      [crio.runtime.runtimes.runc]
        runtime_path = "/usr/bin/runc"
        runtime_type = "oci"
        runtime_root = "/run/runc"
        privileged_without_host_devices = false
  [crio.image]
    default_transport = "docker://"
    global_auth_file = ""

    pause_image_auth_file = ""
    pause_command = "/pause"
    signature_policy = ""
    insecure_registries = []
    image_volumes = "mkdir"
    registries = []
  [crio.network]
    network_dir = "/etc/cni/net.d/"
    plugin_dirs = ["/opt/cni/bin/"]
  [crio.metrics]
    enable_metrics = false
    metrics_port = 9090
```

comments.body: What do I need to do so that podman can manage crio containers

Podman and CRI-O do not share containers. We share most underlying libraries, so images and storage for containers are shared, but Podman and CRI-O do not share a container management library, and so neither can manage the other's containers. As of Podman 3.0, we can do some management (listing them via `podman ps --external`, mounting and unmounting them) but starting, stopping, inspecting are not possible. The intention of the project was originally to replace CRI-O's container creation logic with our own, but the divergent goals of both projects mean this hasn't happened in three years and is unlikely to happen at all.
